title: Sosaties – make them before the holidays are over.
excerpt: Making your own sosaties is a real treat. It's not difficult at all and it stays a winner at the braai. I cut up rump steak, but you can also make it with lamb, pork or chicken. Do give it a try. YOU'LL NEED: sosatie sticks 2,5 kg rump steak 3 onions 1 …

seo.title: Sosaties - make them before the holidays are over. - Marinda Kook

html:
Making your own sosaties is a real treat. It's not difficult at all and it stays a winner at the braai. I cut up rump steak, but you can also make it with lamb, pork or chicken. Do give it a try.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • sosatie sticks
  • 2,5 kg rump steak
  • 3 onions
  • 1 green pepper
  • 2 star anise
  • 3 cardamom seeds
  • 15 ml jeera (cumin)
  • 20 ml turmeric powder
  • 60 ml curry powder
  • 10 ml fine ginger powder
  • 30 ml oil
  • 60 ml sugar
  • 500 ml vinegar
  • 125 ml apricot jam
  • 3 lemon leaves – if you have them
  • salt and pepper
  • 250 ml water

NOW:

  1. soak the sosatie sticks in water for about 30 min
  2. fry two onions that you've sliced into rings in oil until glassy, together with the jeera
  3. cut the other onions and green pepper into pieces and set aside – you're going to thread these onto the stick together with the meat
  4. mix the rest of the ingredients and simmer for 15 minutes, then let it cool – taste the sauce. If it's too sour, add more sugar, too sweet, add more vinegar.
  5. cut your meat into neat cubes and drop into the cold curry sauce. Let it sit in the sauce overnight.
  6. now thread meat, onion, and green pepper in turn onto the stick
  7. pack into a plastic or enamel container and pour the sauce over. Let it sit in the sauce for at least 1 to 3 days.
  8. braai over moderate coals – take care not to let them overcook.

A sosatie with a cold glass of wine in the Boland is a winner.
